name: academic-writer
tagline: 27 specialist skills covering the complete academic writing lifecycle
description: A comprehensive academic writing plugin for Claude Code. Covers every stage of the scholarly writing process: planning paper structure, conducting literature reviews, building arguments, formatting citations in 9 styles, writing abstracts, polishing language and grammar, handling AI ethics disclosures, writing grant proposals (NIH, NSF, EU Horizon, ERC), preparing research proposals, writing lab reports, responding to peer review, reporting statistics (APA 7th, effect sizes, Bayesian), writing LaTeX documents, checking figure and table compliance, selecting journals and detecting predatory publishers, verifying reference accuracy, writing funder-compliant data management plans, crafting journal submission cover letters, reporting qualitative research, writing theses and dissertations, creating plain-language summaries, preregistering research studies, designing conference presentations and posters, and preparing academic CVs and job applications. Suitable for students, researchers, academics, and grant writers across all disciplines.

skills.name: academic-writing-core
skills.description: Master orchestrator — routes to specialist skills; applies discipline-specific conventions

skills.name: abstract-writer
skills.description: Write and improve academic abstracts for journals, dissertations, and conferences

skills.name: academic-language-toolkit
skills.description: Hedging language, reporting verbs, transitions, nominalization, AWL vocabulary

skills.name: ai-academic-ethics
skills.description: Ethical AI use, disclosure requirements, citation of AI, detection tools, institutional policies

skills.name: argumentation-engine
skills.description: Toulmin, Rogerian, Classical models; thesis construction; fallacies; counterargument

skills.name: citation-formatter
skills.description: Generate and fix citations in APA, MLA, Chicago, IEEE, Vancouver, Harvard, ACS, CSE

skills.name: grammar-style-checker
skills.description: Voice, tense, parallel structure, pronoun reference, punctuation, wordiness reduction

skills.name: literature-review
skills.description: Narrative and systematic reviews, PRISMA, synthesis vs. summary, research gaps

skills.name: paper-structure
skills.description: IMRaD, dissertations, essays, case studies, technical reports, policy briefs
triggers: what sections does a research paper need

skills.name: paraphrasing-engine
skills.description: Paraphrase, summarise, quote correctly; avoid plagiarism and patchwriting

skills.name: grant-proposal
skills.description: NIH, NSF, EU Horizon, ERC, Wellcome, UKRI; Specific Aims; Broader Impacts; budget justification

skills.name: research-proposal
skills.description: PhD proposals, ethics submissions, research questions, theoretical frameworks, Gantt charts

skills.name: lab-report
skills.description: Full lab report structure for all sciences; statistical reporting; figure/table formatting

skills.name: peer-review-response
skills.description: Rebuttal letters, point-by-point responses, handling contradictory reviewers, appeals

skills.name: statistical-reporting
skills.description: APA 7th statistical formats (t, F, r, χ²), effect sizes (Cohen's d, η², r), power analysis, Bayesian reporting, CONSORT/STROBE/PRISMA guidelines
triggers: how do I report a t-test

skills.name: latex-writer
skills.description: Document classes, essential packages, BibLaTeX/BibTeX bibliography management, equation formatting, figures and tables in LaTeX, journal templates, error fixes

skills.name: figure-table-checker
skills.description: APA and journal-specific figure/table compliance, error bar selection, colorblind-accessible palettes, CONSORT/PRISMA flow diagrams, common figure mistakes

skills.name: journal-selection
skills.description: Journal metrics (IF, CiteScore, SJR), open access models, predatory journal detection (THINK.CHECK.SUBMIT), journal matching workflow, scope assessment, resubmission strategy
triggers: which journal should I submit to

skills.name: reference-verifier
skills.description: DOI verification via CrossRef, retraction checking via RetractionWatch, AI-hallucinated citation detection, PubPeer integrity checks, pre-submission reference audit

skills.name: data-management-plan
skills.description: NIH DMS Policy, NSF DMP, UKRI, EU Horizon Europe requirements; FAIR principles; repository selection (Zenodo, Dryad, OSF); metadata standards; sensitive data handling

skills.name: cover-letter-writer
skills.description: Journal submission cover letters, presubmission inquiries, letter to editor format, reviewer suggestions, ethics declarations

skills.name: qualitative-reporting
skills.description: COREQ, SRQR guidelines; thematic analysis, grounded theory, phenomenology; reflexivity statements; trustworthiness; qualitative sampling and saturation

skills.name: thesis-dissertation-guide
skills.description: PhD and master's thesis structure, chapter organisation, examiner expectations, viva preparation, country-specific requirements

skills.name: plain-language-summary
skills.description: Lay summaries, plain-language abstracts, funder-mandated summaries (Wellcome, NIHR, EU Horizon), patient summaries, press releases, policy briefs
triggers: explain my research to the public

skills.name: preregistration-guide
skills.description: OSF, AsPredicted, PROSPERO registration; registered reports; hypothesis registration; pre-analysis plans; clinical trial registration

skills.name: conference-presentation
skills.description: Research posters, oral presentations, slide design, conference abstracts; LaTeX beamer and beamerposter; Q&A preparation

skills.name: academic-cv
skills.description: Academic CVs, research statements, teaching philosophies, diversity statements, cover letters for faculty positions, tenure dossiers
triggers: help me with my research statement
